name: Potential DDoS Against Single Host (TCP)
notes: Detects when more than 500 hosts in one minute send packets to a single destination and there is no response.  The number of hosts in this rule can be adjusted for the given environment.  This can indicate an in progress DDoS or a busy service that has become unresponsive.
conditions_text: when the number of source hosts is greater than 500
when the flow context is Remote to Local, Remote to Remote
when the IP protocol is one of the following TCP
when at least 3 flows are seen with the same Destination IP, Destination Port in 5 minutes
thresholds: when at least 3 flows are seen with the same Destination IP, Destination Port in 5 minutes
TD_list: APPLY Potential DDoS Against Single Host (TCP) on flows which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the number of source hosts is greater than 500
AND when the flow context is Remote to Local, Remote to Remote
AND when the IP protocol is one of the following TCP
AND when at least 3 flows are seen with the same Destination IP, Destination Port in 5 minutes
RED_list: Event name: Potential DDoS Against Single Host (TCP)
Event description: More than 500 hosts in one minute sent packets to a single destination with no response.  This can indicate an in progress DDoS or a busy service that has become unresponsive.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Destination IP
This information should not contribute to the naming of the associated offense(s)
